name: @chbrain/khai-engine-moral-judgment
description: khai engine content, the moral-judgment domain: the moral lens a persona reads another's conduct through -- which foundation lights first when they judge right and wrong in someone else.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Moral judgment as position: the moral lens a persona reads another's conduct through -- which of the six foundations lights first when they weigh right and wrong in someone else.

card.wire: Attaches at Position. The anchor (position_moral_judgment.md) declares the moral lens a persona reads another's conduct through -- a standing set of foundations, one of which fires first and lands a verdict before the reasons are gathered. The law makes every persona carry one.
card.issue: Six ready expressions a persona may hold, the six moral foundations: care (against harm), fairness (against cheating), loyalty (against betrayal), authority (against subversion), sanctity (against degradation), and liberty (against oppression). Each is a full position file the build composes onto the anchor, so the persona gets a lens that weighs every other's act, not a label. More expressions slot in as sibling files without moving the anchor.
card.require: Two altitudes must hold. One, the law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter ('personas read others through a moral lens'), so the verdict-first reading holds system-wide and no persona meets another's conduct on neutral ground. Two, the link: each persona carries, under Projection, the foundation that fires first for it. A persona may weight several foundations, but one leads, and the gap between the foundation it leads with and the reasons it gives after is the character, not a contradiction to reject.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared, and each persona links a real moral foundation under Projection. Whether the leading foundation and the persona's stated reasons cohere into a true character is semantic, so it is taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite. The engine ships its own structural tests; the reading is guided, not gated.
card.setup: Two steps. Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then give each persona its leading foundation under Projection: one expression, which carries the anchor upward. Read it in how the persona weighs what another does before the argument arrives; the room reads the verdict beneath the reasons, never narrates it.
